no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md specifies very precise implementation details: use createStore (not createSignal), wrap with persistence from a 'persist' module, use .workspace() API with sdk.directory scoping, retain specific fields (items, failed, paused, edit). These collectively describe exactly WHAT to change and HOW, leaving very little reasoning for the developer. The instruction essentially describes the gold patch structure.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_followup_store_wrapped_with_persistence requires imports from a module with 'persist' in the name AND checks that a persist function is called near createStore+followup. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests use text/regex analysis of the source file (e.g., re.search on file contents). No test actually executes the component, renders it, or verifies runtime behavior. The file explicitly states 'SolidJS components cannot execute without a browser DOM. All tests use file/AST analysis.' Every test is essentially 'assert pattern in open(path).read()' with regex variations.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (test_followup_store_wrapped_with_persistence and test_persistence_workspace_scoped) check for persistence wrapping and workspace scoping that don't exist in the base commit. A stub or no-op change would not add persist imports or .workspace() calls, so these tests would fail on the unmodified file.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors mentioned in instruction.md are tested: persistence wrapping (test 2), workspace scoping (test 3), store fields (test 5), createStore usage (test 8), no any type (test 7), no try/catch/else/for (truncated test), default export (test 4).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.
